name: Napa Kimchee (Jeff Smith, the Frugal Gourmet)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove any limp outer leaves from the cabbage.
2. Quarter the cabbage lengthwise, then cut across into 1 1/2 inch-wide pieces.
3. Place the cabbage in a very large bowl and add the pickling salt. Toss to ensure the salt coats the cabbage evenly. Allow to stand for 30 minutes. Toss the cabbage a couple of times during this time.
4. Rinse the cabbage with cold water, then drain thoroughly.
5. Add the remaining ingredients (carrot, ginger, garlic, candied ginger, sugar, red pepper flakes, and salt) to the cabbage and toss until combined.
6. Pack the mixture into a large crock or covered pottery casserole, adding enough water to cover, about 3 cups. Allow to sit at room temperature for 1 to 2 days.
7. Store in the refrigerator, covered, in the crock or individual glass jars.
Serve as a relish with any Korean dinner or use in cooking meat or soup dishes.
